title: Bridge Essay: The promises and pitfalls of functional magnetic resonance imaging hyperscanning for social interaction research.
concept: Neural synchrony — interpersonal brain-to-brain coupling, hyperscanning, joint music-making, collective entrainment.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Ritual** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Neural synchrony — interpersonal brain-to-brain coupling, hyperscanning, joint music-making, collective entrainment.

**The promises and pitfalls of functional magnetic resonance imaging hyperscanning for social interaction research.** - Soc Personal Psychol Compass (2022). DOI: 10.1111/spc3.12707. PMID: 36407123. Social neuroscience combines tools and perspectives from social psychology and neuroscience to understand how people interact with their social world. Here we discuss a relatively new method-hyperscanning-to study real-time, interactive social interactions using functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI). We highlight three contributions that fMRI hyperscanning makes to the study of the social mind: (1) Naturalism: it shifts the focus from tightly-controlled stimuli to more naturalistic social
